refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these obligations are separated into distinct sub-FRs. Additionally, the FR is stated in terms of what the system must do (derive requirements) without implying how it should be done.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can identify and specify performance requirements based solely on the worksite mission context, independent of any recording or management functions.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall record the derived performance requirements.
The NGHE solution shall manage the recorded performance requirements over time.

candidate_dps: Define a performance requirements derivation process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets
Establish a traceable documentation framework linking worksite inputs to derived performance requirements
Implement a baseline and calibration update management system to maintain history
Integrate safety plan and operational policy constraints into performance requirement derivation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a performance requirements derivation process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets

potential_sources: Tight integration between mission context inputs and performance target outputs
Inclusion of safety and operational policies as constraints
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates the derivation process from documentation and history management, supporting modularity and traceability. Safety and policy constraints are inputs to the derivation process, maintaining independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability and accuracy of worksite mission context inputs
Risk that changes in safety plans or policies require updates to derivation process
Potential complexity in maintaining traceability if inputs or policies change frequently
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating documentation and history management DPs to enhance modularity and traceability in future refinements.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic and processing capabilities, including performance requirements derivation based on mission inputs.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission and worksite data inputs necessary for deriving performance targets.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives tasking commands and mission parameters to inform performance requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication with external systems for data exchange relevant to performance derivation.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as performance derivation is a core system function; minimal diffusion as inputs come through defined interfaces.
Vehicle module is more focused on physical control and actuation; performance derivation would require extensive data sharing and cause diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; performance derivation is not central and would cause diffusion.
Safety Services focus on safety monitoring and alerts; performance derivation is outside its scope, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the performance requirements derivation process because it centralizes system-level logic and interfaces directly with mission inputs, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may grow if too many functions are centralized, risking module bloat.
Interfaces must be well-defined to prevent performance derivation logic from leaking into other modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly defines a process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets, directly addressing the FR requirement to derive performance requirements from the mission context. The mapping is logically sound and traceable.

diffusion_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may grow if too many functions are centralized, risking module bloat.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating good cohesion and minimal scattering of the DP across multiple modules. Inputs come through well-defined interfaces, reducing diffusion risk. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module interfaces with telemetry, tasking, and network link inputs, enabling traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs and maintenance of baseline histories as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear since the NGHE Solution module encompasses the system logic for performance derivation, aligning ownership and responsibility with the FR and DP.

recommended_revisions: Monitor NGHE Solution module complexity to avoid bloat by considering sub-modularization if needed.
Ensure interfaces are rigorously defined and enforced to prevent leakage of performance derivation logic into other modules.
Document traceability links explicitly within the NGHE Solution module to support verification and maintenance.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical mappings, good containment, low diffusion, and feasible verification. The NGHE Solution module is well-defined as the implementation boundary with clear ownership and traceability. Although there is a risk of module bloat due to centralization, this is a monitoring and management issue rather than a need for further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and verified module boundary is paramount, and current decomposition suffices.

revise.why: Monitor NGHE Solution module complexity to avoid bloat by considering sub-modularization if needed; ensure interfaces are rigorously defined and traceability is documented.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can handle current functional complexity without immediate sub-decomposition.
Interfaces and traceability documentation will be maintained rigorously.
open_risks: Potential complexity growth in NGHE Solution module leading to bloat.
Risk of performance derivation logic leakage if interfaces are not enforced.
evidence_needed: Ongoing monitoring reports on module complexity.
Verification records showing traceability and interface enforcement.
